title: centaurea × pantui
intro: Centaurea × pantui, a hybrid plant also known as Perennial Cornflower, is a cross between Centaurea montana and Centaurea cyanus, combining the characteristics of both parent plants. The leaves and flowers of Centaurea × pantui have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Centaurea × pantui is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
